items.headline: Two-time AL Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal to have elbow surgery to remove loose bodies
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Two-time American League Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal will have surgery to remove loose bodies from his elbow after being scratched from Monday's start against the Boston Red Sox.
Detroit Tigers manager A.J. Hinch told reporters that the left-hander felt discomfort in his elbow on Sunday, less than a week after an injury scare against the Atlanta Braves. The 29-year-old — set to become a free agent next offseason — underwent tests that showed the loose bodies. The surgery hadn't yet been scheduled.

items.headline: John Sterling, theatrical Yankees broadcaster known for enduring home run calls, dies at 87
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — John Sterling, the longtime radio broadcaster known for extravagant, individualized home run calls and declaring "theee Yankees win!” after each of New York's victories, died Monday. He was 87.
Sterling had a heart attack and bypass surgery this winter and had returned to his home in Edgewater, New Jersey, where he was cared for by health aides. He died Monday at Englewood Hospital, according to his former wife, Jennifer.

items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Pete Rose became the youngest and 14th player with 3,000 hits
items.description:
May 5
1904 — Cy Young of the Red Sox pitched a perfect game against the Philadelphia Athletics, beating Rube Waddell 3-0. Having pitched nine hitless innings in two previous efforts, he ran his string of hitless innings to 18.

items.headline: Nearly half of Munetaka Murakami's hits have been homers. It takes a lot to sustain that
items.description:
After three straight 100-loss seasons — including a record 121 defeats in 2024 — the Chicago White Sox needed a little excitement.
Munetaka Murakami has certainly provided that.

items.headline: Wrobleski throws 6 scoreless innings as Dodgers snap 4-game slide with victory over Cardinals
items.description:
ST. LOUIS (AP) — Justin Wrobleski tossed six scoreless innings to help the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the St. Louis Cardinals 4-1 on Sunday to snap a four-game losing streak.
Wrobleski (5-0) allowed six hits and a walk. Tanner Scott pitched a scoreless ninth for his second save of the season.

items.headline: Torkelson's home run, McGonigle's RBI singles lift Tigers past Rangers, 7-1
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Spencer Torkelson hit a two-run homer and the Detroit Tigers pulled away to a 7-1 victory over the Texas Rangers on Sunday night.
Torkelson had gone five games without a homer after tying a franchise record by homering in five consecutive games.

items.headline: Anthony Volpe's rehab ends and Yankees option shortstop to Triple-A with José Caballero playing well
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe’s rehab from left shoulder surgery ended Sunday, but the shortstop’s next step is not rejoining the New York Yankees.
With José Caballero excelling as New York's everyday shortstop, the AL East-leading Yankees announced they had optioned Volpe to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

items.headline: Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani goes hitless in 4th straight game for the 1st time since 2022
items.description:
ST. LOUIS (AP) — Shohei Ohtani has gone hitless in four straight games, something that hasn't happened in four years.
Los Angeles Dodgers manager Dave Roberts said Sunday after a 4-1 win over the St. Louis Cardinals that he believes in his star to “come out of it at some point.”

items.headline: Gibson works 4 2/3 innings as the 1st Orioles starting pitcher to debut at Yankee Stadium
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Long before the Orioles let another game against the New York Yankees slip away, Baltimore starter Trey Gibson enjoyed a memorable major league debut.
Gibson worked 4 2/3 innings Sunday and became the first Oriole starting pitcher to make his big league debut at Yankee Stadium since the franchise moved from St. Louis following the 1953 season.

items.headline: The Pittsburgh Pirates believe they're built differently this year. A sweep of Cincy offered proof
items.description:
PITTSBURGH (AP) — Paul Skenes stood at his locker in a near-silent Pittsburgh clubhouse on Thursday after a four-game home sweep at the hands of St. Louis and offered a prediction when asked if he was confident things wouldn't snowball on a Pirates team still finding its way.
“I think we'll be alright,” the reigning NL Cy Young winner offered.
